item.title: GCP adapter JSON.parse on service account token missing try-catch
item.intent: A malformed GCP service account token produces a clear validation error immediately — never an unhandled exception mid-provisioning.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/providers/gcp.ts:132 calls JSON.parse(token) without a try-catch. If the user pastes a malformed service account JSON (missing brace, extra comma), the adapter throws an unhandled exception with a confusing stack trace instead of a validation error. Steps: (1) wrap JSON.parse in try-catch; (2) rethrow as descriptive Error: 'Invalid GCP service account JSON: <original message>'; (3) add test for malformed JSON (empty string, truncated JSON, wrong type). Scope: ~8 lines in gcp.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Malformed JSON token throws with message starting 'Invalid GCP service account JSON:' | Stack trace from JSON.parse never surfaces to caller | Valid service account JSON parses correctly — no regression | Empty string token rejected with clear message
Malformed JSON token throws Error with message starting with 'Invalid GCP service account JSON:'
Empty string token is rejected with clear message
Valid service account JSON parses correctly — no regression on valid GCP paths
Internal JSON.parse stack trace never surfaces to the caller
